sections.paragraph: pl. f. bands, fillets, especially those worn by the Jews at prayers (תְּפִלִּין, φυλακτήρια, Mat 23:5), i.e. scrolls of parchment with sentences written on them out of the law of Moses ( -Ex. 13:110 , -1116 ; -Deu. 6:49 ; -11:1321 ), which the Jews have been accustomed to wear at prayers bound to the forehead and the left wrist, Exo 13:16 Deu 6:8 11:18 . [It requires proof that the Jewish phylacteries are here intended by these fillets or bandages.] (Ch. טֹוטְפָא, טֹוטַפְתָּא a bracelet, a frontlet. This word is for טָפְטָפָה, like בָּבֶל for בַּלְבֵּל, כּ74כָב for כָּבְכָּב, גֻּלְגֹּלֶת, Syr. ܓܘܽ ܓܳܠܬܐܳ, Lehrg. p. 869. Root טוּף, which see; and not טפף, to which the signification of binding has been hastily attributed.)
